claim: Syriac āsyā ('physician') dominates JBA rappā 37:1, confirming primarily Syriac rather than JBA tradition.
evidence_for: Small absolute counts but large ratio
Pharmaceutical text should contain physician vocabulary
evidence_against: Absolute count of rappā (1) is below statistical significance threshold
test: Count skeletons '>sy' and 'rp' in corpus; verify 37:1 directivity
tests_remaining: Count skeletons '>sy' and 'rp' in corpus; verify 37:1 directivity
source: Brady Defibaugh (April 2026), A Syriac Pharmaceutical Dispensatory Encoded in the Voynich Manuscript (DANI pipeline v31)
